name: Moulor Tower Trail
name_original: Le tour du lac de Loudenvielle
description: Enjoy an easy 3.3-mile hike around Loudenvielle Lake in the Pyrénées, offering panoramic views and family-friendly paths.
region_intro: Hiking around Génos offers access to diverse landscapes within the historic Louron Valley, characterized by its prominent lake, surrounding mountains, and forested areas. The region features a mix of terrain, from gentle paths around the lake to more challenging mountain trails with significant elevation gains. Hikers can explore the natural environment, including the blue waters of Lake Génos-Loudenvielle and the slopes of the Pyrénées. Best hiking trails around Génos The most popular hiking route is Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Loudenvielle Lake loop from Génos, an easy 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering accessible paths around the lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Soula Refuge – Lac de Caillauas loop from Loudenvielle, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and alpine terrain. Local hikers also love the Moulor Tower Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain to historic watchtowers, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Hiking around Génos is defined by mountainous terrain, the serene Lake Génos-Loudenvielle, and forest walks, often featuring historic landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to demanding mountain treks. The routes in Génos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 7,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Génos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Génos?
A: Génos, located in the heart of the Louron Valley, offers a diverse range of hiking experiences. You'll find everything from easy, accessible paths around the serene Lake Génos-Loudenvielle to more challenging mountain treks with significant elevation gains in the surrounding Pyrénées. The area also features pleasant forest walks and trails leading to historical landmarks.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking options in Génos?
A: Yes, Génos is very welcoming for families and those seeking easier walks. The paths around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15016927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle&lt;/a&gt; are particularly accessible, offering panoramic views and a gentle 4.3 km loop that is suitable for all skill levels. There are 11 easy routes available in the area.

Q: What are some challenging hikes near Génos for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers looking for a challenge, the region offers demanding routes with significant elevation. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24679347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soula Refuge – Lac de Caillauas loop from Loudenvielle&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 15.7 km path that features alpine terrain and substantial climbs. There are 9 difficult routes in total around Génos.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking in Génos?
A: As you explore the area, you can discover intriguing historical buildings and elevated viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877595887/moulor-tower-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulor Tower Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads to the 12th-century Moulor Tower, offering cultural interest and great views. The 13th-century Génos Tower is another significant historical site on the heights surrounding the lake.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Génos?
A: Yes, Génos features several circular routes perfect for exploring the area. The popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15016927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Loudenvielle Lake loop from Génos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular path. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10532334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Génos-Loudenvielle – Loudenvielle Lake loop from Génos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.1 km circular route that offers a slightly longer experience around the lake.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Génos?
A: The hiking trails around Génos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the serene beauty of Lake Génos-Loudenvielle, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on a hike in Génos?
A: Génos is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter the stunning blue waters of Lake Génos-Loudenvielle, which often creates a beautiful mirror effect reflecting the surrounding mountains. The trails also lead through lush forest areas and offer panoramic views of the enchanting Louron Valley and the majestic Pyrénées mountain range.

Q: Are there any places to relax after a day of hiking in Génos?
A: Absolutely! After a rewarding day on the trails, you can unwind at the nearby Balnéa, the largest spa in the French Pyrenees. It offers a perfect opportunity for relaxation with its baths, hammams, saunas, and massages, just a short distance from Génos.

Q: Can I find mountain passes or high-altitude viewpoints near Génos?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Génos provides access to several impressive passes and high-altitude viewpoints. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Val Louron-Azet&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/610188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Esquierry Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which offer breathtaking vistas of the surrounding peaks and valleys.

Q: Are there any lakes or refuges accessible from Génos for longer hikes?
A: For those interested in longer excursions, several beautiful lakes and refuges are accessible. You can explore destinations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5186545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Oô&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2600839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Espingo and Espingo Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/555882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estós Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for hikers seeking more remote and scenic experiences.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Génos area?
A: There are over 30 hiking routes available around Génos, catering to a wide range of preferences and fitness levels. This includes 11 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

There are a whole bunch of activities all around the lake, pump track, canoeing, tree climbing, balnea, swimming pool, river etc...

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike around Lake Loudenvielle?
faqs.answer: The hike around Loudenvielle Lake is an easy stroll that typically takes about 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. It's a gentle route perfect for a relaxed outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tour of Lake Loudenvielle is considered easy and is very suitable for beginners, families with children, and even strollers. The path is largely flat and accessible, making it a great option for all skill levels.

faqs.question: Is the route a loop, or an out-and-back trail?
faqs.answer: The trail around Loudenvielle Lake is a loop, allowing you to enjoy continuous new scenery without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting Lake Loudenvielle for the hike?
faqs.answer: There are several parking options available around Loudenvielle Lake. You'll find designated parking areas conveniently located near the start of the trail, making access straightforward.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mostly flat and accessible, with stabilized paths in many sections. While generally even, some parts might be less smooth. It's a gentle walk around the lake, suitable for various users.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike around Lake Loudenvielle?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring, summer, and early autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking and enjoying the surrounding activities. In winter, you might experience snow, adding a different charm.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! You'll be treated to incredible and panoramic views of the beautiful Loudenvielle Lake itself, often reflecting the surrounding Pyrenees peaks. There's also a fantastic Panorama of the valley. For a diversion, consider the climb to the 13th-century Tour de Génos for stunning elevated views, though the path is steep.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of the route overlap with other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.5, Val d'Aube 8, and Route du Château.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Lake Loudenvielle dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the trail around Loudenvielle Lake. Please ensure they are kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to access the trail or the Vallée du Louron?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Tour of Lake Loudenvielle or to access the general area of the Vallée du Louron. It's freely accessible for everyone to enjoy.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or cafes available near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the charming village of Loudenvielle is right next to the lake and offers various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities, including public restrooms, making it convenient for visitors.
